Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT TIMOTHY JENKINS, CARMEN JENKINS, MONICA JENKINS Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is DENIED without prejudice. Motion contains no evidence upon which the Court may base a determination that the delay in filing a cross-complaint was in good faith. No facts supporting oversight, inadvertence, mistake, neglect provided. Conclusory, self-serving declaration of counsel without facts is insufficient. The Court notes that no proposed cross-complaint was attached to the motion and no reply was filed. The Court notes that the moving party failed to comply with SFLR 2.B. Failure to comply in the future will result in motions being taken off calendar. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File X-Complt
Off Calendar May-19-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances by any attorney, any party or any interested party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR, insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1013. The Court further notes that contrary to the assertion in the moving papers no proposed cross-complaint was attached to the papers (Motion 1:20). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Motion And Notice Of Hearing (Motions)
